resolvedQuestions.summary: Distinctive capabilities surveyed across v0, Lovable, Figma Make, Subframe, and adjacent tools.
Deduped against packet 005; net-new patterns isolated from Claude Design parity overlap.
ADOPT / ADAPT / SKIP verdicts recorded per local skill.
Negative knowledge captured for hosted, platform, preset, and over-automation directions.
Prioritized recommendation set produced for merge host.

keyFindings.title: Pre-build direction gate
keyFindings.summary: Lovable and Subframe generate multiple visual directions before committing to a build; adapt as a brief-specific critique gate, not presets.

keyFindings.title: Element-target feedback grammar
keyFindings.summary: v0, Lovable, Figma Make, and Subframe all let users point to/select/annotate a rendered element, then describe a scoped change.

keyFindings.title: Design-system adherence scanner
keyFindings.summary: Lovable explicitly checks for raw colors, one-off values, inline overrides, and local component misuse; Figma Make guidelines document token pitfalls and don't-do rules.

keyFindings.title: Editable plan artifact before build
keyFindings.summary: Figma Make plan mode creates a reviewable plan markdown file before code changes; local skills can borrow the small artifact pattern for complex design work.

keyFindings.title: Presentational/generated boundary
keyFindings.summary: Subframe separates deterministic presentational code from wrapper-owned business logic; mcp-magicpath should make the same boundary explicit for installed/generated components.

keyFindings.title: Guidelines as design-system context
keyFindings.summary: Figma Make kits and Lovable design systems put machine-readable rules near component/tokens; local skills can emit and consume compact guidelines without hosting a platform.

keyFindings.title: Fidelity loop limitations
keyFindings.summary: Browser testing is useful for behavior and screenshots but not subtle color/detail judgment; completion gates should state this limitation.

keyFindings.title: MCP/design-document access
keyFindings.summary: Subframe exposes pages, components, theme, and design documents through MCP; MagicPath remains CLI-only, so adopt the output-manifest idea rather than the server surface.
